name: Sendero del Valle del Bajo Arlanza (PRC-BU 126) — circular desde Escuderos
region_intro: Hiking trails around Santa María Del Campo traverse a landscape characteristic of the Castilian plateau in the Arlanza region of Burgos, Spain. The area is defined by its broad vistas, agricultural fields, and the presence of the Arlanza and Arlanzón rivers. These rivers create natural features and agreeable riverside strolls, contributing to the varied terrain. The region offers routes suitable for exploring its rural charm and natural surroundings. Best hiking trails around Santa María Del Campo The most popular hiking route is Río Arlanza y Villahoz — circular desde Tordómar, a 10.8 miles (17.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Arlanza river valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sendero del Valle del Bajo Arlanza (PRC-BU 126) — circular desde Escuderos, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This trail explores the lower Arlanza valley, featuring varied terrain and natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Villahoz loop from Villahoz, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Hiking around Santa María Del Campo is defined by riverside paths, agricultural fields, and rolling hills of the Castilian plateau. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy and moderate routes. The routes in Santa María Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 10 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa María Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Santa María Del Campo?
A: The hiking trails around Santa María Del Campo are characteristic of the Castilian plateau. You can expect broad vistas, agricultural fields, and rolling hills. The presence of the Arlanza and Arlanzón rivers also means you'll find pleasant riverside paths, offering a varied and scenic experience.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, there are easy hiking options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17542364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villahoz loop from Villahoz&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.6-mile (5.8 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 30 minutes to complete, leading through rural landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: While hiking, you can explore the natural beauty of the Arlanza and Arlanzón rivers, which define much of the landscape. You might also encounter historical points of interest such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5572210" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arlanza River Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presencio&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040988412/rio-arlanza-y-villahoz-circular-desde-tordomar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Arlanza y Villahoz — circular desde Tordómar&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.8-mile (17.4 km) trail offering views of the Arlanza river valley.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Santa María Del Campo?
A: The trails in Santa María Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the agreeable riverside strolls, and the rural charm of the Castilian plateau.

Q: Is there parking available for hikers near the trailheads?
A: As Santa María Del Campo is a rural area, parking is generally available in or near the starting points of the trails, especially in the villages from which many routes begin. It's advisable to check specific village parking options if you're driving to a trailhead.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Most rural trails in Spain, including those around Santa María Del Campo, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural fields or near livestock, and to respect local regulations and private property.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Santa María Del Campo suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, with a mix of easy and moderate routes, there are options suitable for families. The easier, shorter loops, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17542364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villahoz loop from Villahoz&lt;/a&gt;, are great for a family outing, offering gentle terrain and rural scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa María Del Campo?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in the Castilian plateau region. The weather is milder, and the landscapes are vibrant with spring blooms or rich autumn colors. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cold, though hiking is possible year-round with appropriate gear.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest near the trails?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, the region is rich in history. You can explore charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518073" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villahoz&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5473710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tordómar&lt;/a&gt;, which often feature traditional architecture and historical churches, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5732883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Our Lady of the Assumption of Villahoz&lt;/a&gt;, blending cultural exploration with your hike.

Q: How can I reach the hiking areas by public transport?
A: Public transport options to Santa María Del Campo and surrounding villages might be limited, especially for direct access to specific trailheads. It's advisable to check local bus schedules from larger towns in the Burgos province, as services may be infrequent, particularly on weekends.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking routes?
A: Santa María Del Campo and nearby villages offer a rural charm, and you can typically find local eateries (restaurants or bars) for refreshments and traditional Spanish cuisine. For accommodation, there might be guesthouses or rural hotels available, especially in the larger villages, providing a comfortable base for your hiking adventures.

Q: What should I wear or bring for hiking in this region?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for sun exposure on the Castilian plateau, it's recommended to wear comfortable hiking shoes, layered clothing, and sun protection (hat, sunscreen). Always carry enough water, snacks, a map or GPS device, and a fully charged phone for safety.
tips: Roman bridge over the Arlanza river, next to a small district of Escuderos. Beautiful views of the river, here the Lower Arlanza Valley trail begins.

Part of the path that passes along the banks of the Arlanza, which are covered with a well-preserved riverside forest, with the presence of willows, poplars, poplars, alders and a great variety of birds.

As we climb to the highest part of the Lower Arlanza Valley Trail along a dirt road, we can enjoy the views of the fields on one side and the forest on the other. A place that transmits peace and tranquility.
